scaling_sensitivity
prediction.rationale: The proposed action runs logistic regression without scaling and with C=0.1. Given that unscaled logistic regression previously achieved ~0.951 accuracy and scaled versions with C=0.1 or C=1.0 achieved ~0.979-0.986, this unscaled, regularized variant is unlikely to improve upon the best observed score. It also does not address the open question of robustness across seeds or perturbations, so it is unlikely to change what the final report can claim.
prediction.action_specific_signal: unscaled logistic regression with C=0.1 is expected to perform near the unscaled baseline (~0.951) and below the best scaled runs (~0.986).
prediction.claim_risk: single-run best score may not justify a scientific claim
prediction.why_this_action_changes_claims: This action is unlikely to change what the final report can claim because it repeats an unscaled configuration that has already been shown to underperform scaled variants and does not test robustness across seeds or perturbations.
prediction.why_this_action_may_be_wasteful: Running unscaled logistic regression with C=0.1 is likely to yield a score similar to or worse than the existing unscaled run (run_001) and significantly worse than the best scaled runs, providing no new evidence for robust claims.
prediction.stop_condition: If the goal is to make robust claims about the best model, continue with robustness verification (e.g., top_model_verification or label-noise sweeps) rather than adding more single-run unscaled logistic regression experiments.
